00038 
00039 package java.io;
00040 
00041 import java.lang.RuntimeException;
00042 import java.lang.System;
00043 
00069 public class ByteArrayOutputStream extends OutputStream
00070 {
00074     protected byte[] buf;
00075 
00079     protected int count;
00080 
00084     private boolean closed;
00085 
00089     private static final int DEFAULT_INITIAL_BUFFER_SIZE = 32;
00090 
00100     public ByteArrayOutputStream()
00101     {
00102         this (DEFAULT_INITIAL_BUFFER_SIZE);
00103     }
00104 
00112     public ByteArrayOutputStream (int size) throws IllegalArgumentException
00113     {
00114         if (size < 0)
00115             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00116 
00117         buf = new byte[size];
00118         count = 0;
00119         closed = false;
00120     }
00121 
00129     public synchronized void write (int oneByte) throws IOException
00130     {
00131         if (closed)
00132             throw new IOException();
00133 
00134         resize (1);
00135         buf[count++] = (byte) oneByte;
00136     }
00137 
00148     public synchronized void write (byte[] buffer, int offset, int add)
00149         throws IOException
00150     {
00151         if (closed)
00152             throw new IOException();
00153 
00154         // If ADD < 0 then arraycopy will throw the appropriate error for us.
00155         if (add >= 0)
00156             resize (add);
00157 
00158         System.arraycopy(buffer, offset, buf, count, add);
00159         count += add;
00160     }
00161 
00168     public synchronized void reset()
00169     {
00170         if (closed)
00171             throw new RuntimeException();
00172 
00173         count = 0;
00174     }
00175 
00184     public synchronized byte[] toByteArray()
00185     {
00186         // If the stream is closed so the internal buffer is immutable, we can
00187         // use it instead of creating a new one, this will save precious
00188         // memory. Otherwise we have to create a new array
00189         if (!closed)
00190         {
00191             byte[] ret = new byte[count];
00192 
00193             System.arraycopy(buf, 0, ret, 0, count);
00194 
00195             return ret;
00196         }
00197         else
00198             return buf;
00199     }
00200 
00213     public int size()
00214     {
00215         return count;
00216     }
00217 
00227     public String toString ()
00228     {
00229         return new String (buf, 0, count);
00230     }
00231 
00239     public synchronized void close() throws IOException
00240     {
00241         closed = true;
00242     }
00243 
00244     // Resize buffer to accommodate new bytes.
00245     private void resize (int add)
00246     {
00247         if (count + add > buf.length)
00248         {
00249             int newlen = buf.length * 2;
00250 
00251             if (count + add > newlen)
00252                 newlen = count + add;
00253 
00254             byte[] newbuf = new byte[newlen];
00255 
00256             System.arraycopy(buf, 0, newbuf, 0, count);
00257             buf = newbuf;
00258         }
00259     }
00260 }
